description: Untrusted input is concatenated into a SQL, shell, path, or template sink without parameterization, enabling injection.
body: Failure mode: request data is interpolated directly into a query string, command line, file path, or template, so crafted input changes the statement's structure and executes attacker intent. Detection signals: string concatenation building SQL/shell; injection payloads in a negative test alter behavior; scanners flag a tainted-data flow to a sink. Do not rely on denylist sanitization or manual escaping. Safer fallback: validate against an allowlist at the boundary and use parameterized queries, argument arrays for subprocesses, and safe path/template APIs; verify with injection regression tests.
